item.title: Shark kills spearfisher in front of friends: "Terrifying thing to see"
item.description: A spearfisher was killed on the Great Barrier Reef on Sunday in Australia's second fatal shark attack in just over a week, police said.

item.title: Efforts to stop California chemical leak in Orange County on new trajectory, officials say
item.description: More than 50,000 people in California remain evacuated due to an Orange County chemical leak that prompted Gov. Newsom to request that President Trump issue a Federal Emergency Declaration.

item.title: The role genetically engineered mice could play in fighting Lyme disease
item.description: Scientists are hoping to use genetic engineering to reduce the transmission of Lyme disease. The scientists' target is not the deer or the ticks often associated with the disease; it's wild mice.

item.title: Sally Field says she believes "in the resilience of our Constitution" | 60 Minutes
item.description: Sally Field memorized the First Amendment as a child. The Oscar winner says she now understands "it like never before," stressing that "this fragile thing called democracy needs to be protected."

item.title: Andrew Ross Sorkin sees worrying signs in market after months of ups
item.description: Stocks on Wall Street have rallied in recent months, but author Andrew Ross Sorkin sees a crash coming. The question is: when will the bubble pop, and how much will the market slump when it does?

item.title: Inside the fun, accessible method helping piano students win competitions | 60 Minutes
item.description: Payam Khastkhodaei, the son of Iranian immigrants, says he's developed a new method of teaching that has students loving their piano lessons. Now his students are sweeping national competitions.

item.title: Genetically engineering mouse DNA could be key to curbing Lyme disease | 60 Minutes
item.description: Scientists are hoping to use genetic engineering to reduce the transmission of Lyme disease. The scientists' target is not the deer or the ticks often associated with the disease; it's wild mice.

item.title: Applying lessons from the Wall Street crash of 1929 to today's market | 60 Minutes
item.description: Stocks on Wall Street have rallied in recent months, but author Andrew Ross Sorkin sees a crash coming. The question is: when will the bubble pop, and how much will the market slump when it does?

item.title: How the "Payam Method" keeps the joy in learning piano
item.description: From improvising classical music to playing songs students like, the approach by Payam Music focuses on student enjoyment while learning piano.

item.title: Piano teacher's students sweep competitions after learning "Payam Method"
item.description: Payam Khastkhodaei, the son of Iranian immigrants, says he's developed a new method of teaching that has students loving their piano lessons. Now his students are sweeping national competitions.

item.title: How the "Payam Method" keeps the joy in learning piano
item.description: From improvising classical music to playing songs students like, the approach by Payam Music focuses on student enjoyment while learning piano.

item.title: "Payam Method" reimagines music education, piano lessons for kids
item.description: A piano teacher has won over an Oscar-winning composer with his new method for teaching students to play, and to compose their own music. The "Payam Method" has students sweeping national competitions.

item.title: U.S., Iran have agreed to broad principles of deal, Trump official says
item.description: There is broad commitment on the principles of a deal, a senior Trump administration official said, and the administration feels positive about where things stand.

item.title: Iran agrees in principle to dispose of highly-enriched uranium, official says
item.description: Negotiations are "in a very good place," a senior Trump official said, but, a deal to end the Iran war likely will not be signed this weekend.

item.title: Good Night and Good Luck: A CBS News Radio Retrospective
item.description: Before signing off on Friday, May 22, 2026, CBS News Radio looked back at its 99-year history with a retrospective, "Good Night and Good Luck."

item.title: Iran's supreme leader is holed up in undisclosed location, U.S. intel says
item.description: Officials at the highest levels of the Iranian government say they don't know where Mojtaba Khamenei is and have no way to contact him directly, relying instead on a network of couriers.

item.title: Alleged gunman outside White House had run-ins with Secret Service, sources say
item.description: Sources identified the 21-year-old suspect as Nasire Best of Dundalk, Maryland, and documents obtained by CBS News show Best previously blocked a White House entry lane in June 2025.

item.title: This week on "Sunday Morning" (May 24)
item.description: A look at the features for this week's broadcast of the Emmy-winning program, hosted by Lee Cowan.

item.title: Patti LaBelle on being called a legend: "I have earned it"
item.description: Patti LaBelle said she's "having fun living it down" as the legendary singer marks her 82nd birthday.

item.title: Extended interview: Patti LaBelle
item.description: It's been some 50 years since Patti LaBelle first sang her hit song "Lady Marmalade." As she marks her 82nd birthday, LaBelle talks to correspondent Tracy Smith about her career and what's next.

item.title: Full transcript of "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: On this "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" broadcast, Kevin Hassett, White House National Economic Council director, and Sen. Chris Van Hollen, Democrat of Maryland, join Nancy Cordes.

item.title: Where Iran peace talks stand amid Pakistani army chief's meeting in Tehran
item.description: Pakistani Field Marshal Asim Munir met with senior Iranian officials in Tehran on Saturday. Meanwhile, President Trump said on social media that the U.S. and Iran were nearing a peace deal. CBS News' Courtney Kealy has more.

item.title: Iran agrees in principle to dispose of highly-enriched uranium, White House official says
item.description: Iran has agreed to a deal in principle to dispose of its highly-enriched uranium, a senior Trump administration official said Sunday. CBS News' James LaPorta has more.

item.title: What to know about California chemical tank set to explode or leak
item.description: The tank at GKN Aerospace is estimated to contain 7,000 gallons of methyl methacrylate, a volatile chemical used to produce plastics.

item.title: Gunman killed by Secret Service after opening fire outside of White House, officials say
item.description: A gunman was killed after opening fire at a Secret Service checkpoint outside the White House on Saturday night, officials said. CBS News' Nancy Cordes, Nicole Sganga and Natalie Brand have the latest.

item.title: Tehran pushes back on Trump touting potential peace deal that would reopen Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump has signaled on social media that the U.S. and Iran are moving closer to a deal that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz, but Tehran refuted some of the claims. CBS News national security analyst Aaron MacLean has more.

item.title: Transcript: Lt. Col. William Swenson (Ret.) and Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams (Ret.) on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan," May 24, 2026
item.description: The following is the transcript of the interview with retired Lt. Col. William Swenson and retired Command Sergeant Major Matthew Williams (Ret.) that aired on "Face the Nation with Margaret Brennan" on May 24, 2026.

item.title: Iran moving World Cup training base from U.S. to Mexico
item.description: Iran had been scheduled to train in Tucson, Arizona, but it will now move its training base to Tijuana, Mexico, just south of San Diego.

item.title: CBS News Radio signs off after nearly 100 years: "An American institution"
item.description: After nearly a century on the air, CBS News Radio signed off, with the final reports airing on Friday, May 22.

item.title: Judge tosses federal charges against Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: A federal district judge granted Kilmar Abrego Garcia's motion to dismiss two criminal charges on the grounds his prosecution was vindictive and selective.
